titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Matthew Shepperson archive

publicationstmt.publisher: Yale Center for British Art, Rare Books and Manuscripts

address.addressline: Department of Rare Books and Manuscripts

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Yale Center for British Art, Rare Books and Manuscripts

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 31, 2020 at 21:25

corpname.part: Yale Center for British Art, Rare Books and Manuscripts

did.abstract: The collection comprises manuscript material concerning Matthew Shepperson, portrait copyist, art instructor to elite lords and ladies, and collector of portraits

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Matthew Shepperson (1785-1874) attained fair success as a portrait copyist. Shepperson exhibited paintings at the Royal Academy between 1811 and 1821. He painted copies of portraits in his own collection and sold them to numerous lords, ladies, bishops and earls. He also taught painting in circles of nobility, mainly to socially prominent young ladies. Circa 1823-24 he made a copy of John Hoppner's painting of Horatio Nelson, which is now in the collections of the National Maritime Museum, and in 1828 he painted a portrait of John Childs (b. 1784). In August 2010, some of the paintings from Shepperson's collection (bequeathed to his heirs) were sold at auction; the lots includes Velazquez's Portrait of a gentleman, which sold for £3 million.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection comprises manuscript material concerning Matthew Shepperson, portrait copyist, art instructor to elite lords and ladies, and collector of portraits. It includes two account books, recording forty-three years of financial records. Also present are four visiting cards, the original will of Shepperson's mother (naming him as executor and heir), one manuscript receipt for paintings, and 18 autograph letters signed, four of the latter with red wax seals. A number of the letters are from John Childs, dated from about 1823 to 1826.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The collection is the physical property of the Yale Center for British Art. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the Curator of Rare Books and Manuscripts.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open without restriction.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Matthew Shepperson Archive, Yale Center for British Art, Paul Mellon Fund

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Yale Center for British Art, Paul Mellon Fund
